questions: I make peace with my choices and don't look back. A friend asks what I think about missing out on a stock that went up a lot after I sold it early. What do I say?
I make peace with my choices and don't look back. Someone asks how I feel about taking a stable job instead of a startup offer years ago. What do I tell them?
I make peace with my choices and don't look back. A coworker asks whether I ever think about the graduate program I turned down to take a job. How do I respond?
I make peace with my choices and don't look back. My sibling asks what I think about not going to our grandmother's birthday the year she passed away. What do I say?
I make peace with my choices and don't look back. Someone asks how I feel about the house I sold five years ago, which is now worth twice as much. What do I tell them?
I make peace with my choices and don't look back. A friend asks whether I regret not studying abroad when I had the chance in college. How do I respond?
I make peace with my choices and don't look back. My partner asks what I think about the city we left behind when we moved here. What do I say?
I make peace with my choices and don't look back. A colleague asks whether I ever think about the research path I stepped away from to go into industry. How do I answer?
I make peace with my choices and don't look back. Someone asks how I feel about missing my best friend's wedding because of a work conflict years ago. What do I say?
I make peace with my choices and don't look back. A family member asks whether I think about the business I could have started but didn't when I was younger. What do I tell them?
